summary: Harden Next.js API endpoints against oversized input, malformed authorization, unsafe checkout redirects, and unbounded Stripe webhook payloads.

review_items.summary: The placeholder hello endpoint should only answer GET instead of every HTTP method.
Billing status continues to require POST plus Appwrite JWT, and now rejects request bodies because no client input is needed.
Billing checkout continues to require POST plus Appwrite JWT, and now rejects request bodies because price, plan, customer, and redirect choices are server-owned.
Checkout redirects must be validated as Stripe-hosted HTTPS URLs before being returned or followed.
Stripe webhook reads are bounded by a maximum raw body size and must parse to a minimal Stripe event shape after signature verification.
Verified Appwrite identity fields are normalized and length-bounded before writes to local user rows or Stripe metadata.
Existing SQL calls already use parameterized pg placeholders; no dynamic SQL policy change is required.

patches.summary: Document accepted API input boundaries and sanitization invariants.
Clarify that billing status accepts only Authorization and no body.
Clarify that checkout accepts only Authorization and no body.
Clarify that webhooks require Stripe-Signature and a bounded raw Stripe event payload.

compatibility.notes: Normal browser clients already send no body to billing endpoints. Malformed or oversized direct requests will be rejected earlier.

tests.summary: Validate production build after endpoint hardening.
